server.c(3) Library Functions Manual server.c(3)

NAME

server.c - WebSocket server implementation.

SYNOPSIS

Data Structures


struct websocket_connection_data_t
Per-connection user data.

Functions


asciichat_error_t websocket_server_init (websocket_server_t *server, const websocket_server_config_t *config)
asciichat_error_t websocket_server_run (websocket_server_t *server)
void websocket_server_cancel_service (websocket_server_t *server)
void websocket_server_destroy (websocket_server_t *server)

Detailed Description

WebSocket server implementation.

void
  websocket_server_destroy (websocket_server_t * server)


Definition at line 834 of file
    lib/network/websocket/server.c.


834                                                           {
835   if (!server) {
836     return;
837   }
838 
839   atomic_store_bool(&server->running, false);
840 
841   // Cancel any pending libwebsockets service calls to interrupt blocking lws_service()
842   // This prevents the event loop from getting stuck in lws_service()
843   if (server->context) {
844     log_debug("[WEBSOCKET_SERVER_DESTROY] Cancelling libwebsockets service");
845     lws_cancel_service(server->context);
846   }
847 
848   // Unregister atomic fields
849   NAMED_UNREGISTER(&server->running);
850 
851   // Destroy handler thread pool (waits for pending work to complete)
852   if (server->handler_pool) {
853     thread_pool_destroy(server->handler_pool);
854     server->handler_pool = NULL;
855   }
856 
857   // Context is normally destroyed by websocket_server_run (from the event loop
858   // thread) for fast shutdown. This handles the case where run() wasn't called
859   // or didn't complete normally.
860   if (server->context) {
861     NAMED_UNREGISTER(server->context);
862     log_debug("WebSocket context still alive in destroy, cleaning up");
863     lws_context_destroy(server->context);
864     server->context = NULL;
865   }
866 
867   log_debug("WebSocket server destroyed");
868 }






References atomic_store_bool(), and
    thread_pool_destroy().


Referenced by acds_main(), and server_main().






asciichat_error_t
  websocket_server_init (websocket_server_t * server, const
  websocket_server_config_t * config)


Definition at line 677 of file
    lib/network/websocket/server.c.


677                                                                                                              {
678   if (!server || !config || !config->client_handler) {
679     return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, "Invalid parameters");
680   }
681 
682   memset(server, 0, sizeof(*server));
683   server->handler = config->client_handler;
684   server->user_data = config->user_data;
685   server->port = config->port;
686   atomic_store_bool(&server->running, true);
687 
688   // Store server pointer in protocol user data so callbacks can access it
689   websocket_protocols[0].user = server; // http protocol
690   websocket_protocols[1].user = server; // acip protocol
691 
692   // Enable libwebsockets logging through centralized logging system
693   lws_log_init_server();
694 
695   // Configure libwebsockets context
696   struct lws_context_creation_info info = {0};
697   info.port = config->port;
698   info.protocols = websocket_protocols;
699   info.gid = (gid_t)-1;                                // Cast to avoid undefined behavior with unsigned type
700   info.uid = (uid_t)-1;                                // Cast to avoid undefined behavior with unsigned type
701   info.options = LWS_SERVER_OPTION_DO_SSL_GLOBAL_INIT; // Initialize SSL/TLS support (required for server binding)
702   info.extensions = NULL;                              // Disable permessage-deflate - causing connection issues
703   info.retry_and_idle_policy = &keep_alive_policy; // Configure keep-alive to prevent idle disconnects during handshake
704 
705   // Configure TLS/WSS support if certificates are provided (check for non-empty strings)
706   if (config->tls_cert_path && config->tls_cert_path[0] != '\0' && config->tls_key_path &&
707       config->tls_key_path[0] != '\0') {
708 #ifdef LWS_WITH_TLS
709     // Validate certificate and key files are readable
710     if (access(config->tls_cert_path, R_OK) != 0) {
711       return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_NETWORK_BIND, "TLS certificate file not readable: %s (errno=%d)", config->tls_cert_path, errno);
712     }
713     if (access(config->tls_key_path, R_OK) != 0) {
714       return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_NETWORK_BIND, "TLS key file not readable: %s (errno=%d)", config->tls_key_path, errno);
715     }
716 
717     info.ssl_cert_filepath = config->tls_cert_path;
718     info.ssl_private_key_filepath = config->tls_key_path;
719     log_info("WebSocket server configured for WSS (TLS): cert=%s, key=%s", config->tls_cert_path, config->tls_key_path);
720 #else
721     log_warn("WebSocket server: TLS support not compiled in libwebsockets; WSS unavailable");
722 #endif
723   } else if ((config->tls_cert_path && config->tls_cert_path[0] != '\0') ||
724              (config->tls_key_path && config->tls_key_path[0] != '\0')) {
725     log_warn("WebSocket server: Both TLS certificate and key must be provided for WSS; using plain WS");
726   }
727 
728   // Increase per-thread service buffer to prevent fragmentation of large messages
729   // Default is 4KB, causing large frames to fragment into many chunks
730   // Video frames are 921KB, so increase to 2MB to handle them without fragmentation
731   info.pt_serv_buf_size = 2 * 1024 * 1024; // 2MB per-thread service buffer
732 
733   // Disable ALL default timeouts and keep-alive mechanisms
734   // Use only the explicit retry_and_idle_policy we set above (30/35 seconds)
735   info.ka_time = 0;           // Disable TCP keep-alive probes (use WebSocket pings instead)
736   info.ka_probes = 0;         // Disable TCP probes
737   info.ka_interval = 0;       // Disable TCP probe intervals
738   info.keepalive_timeout = 0; // Disable HTTP keep-alive timeout entirely
739 
740   // Explicitly set a very large idle timeout to prevent early disconnection
741   // Without this, libwebsockets defaults to 5 seconds for HTTP connections
742   // Set to effectively infinite (100 hours) since we use retry_and_idle_policy instead
743   info.timeout_secs = 360000; // 100 hours - effectively disabled
744 
745   // Create libwebsockets context
746   // Log diagnostic info before attempting creation
747   log_debug("lws_create_context: port=%u, protocols=%p, options=0x%x, ssl_cert=%s, ssl_key=%s",
748             info.port, (void*)info.protocols, info.options,
749             info.ssl_cert_filepath ? info.ssl_cert_filepath : "(none)",
750             info.ssl_private_key_filepath ? info.ssl_private_key_filepath : "(none)");
751 
752   server->context = lws_create_context(&info);
753   if (!server->context) {
754     // Capture any OpenSSL errors that may have occurred during context creation
755     unsigned long ssl_err = 0;
756     char ssl_err_str[256] = "no OpenSSL errors in queue";
757 
758     // Extract all errors from OpenSSL error queue
759     while ((ssl_err = ERR_get_error()) != 0) {
760       ERR_error_string_n(ssl_err, ssl_err_str, sizeof(ssl_err_str));
761       log_error("OpenSSL error: %lu: %s", ssl_err, ssl_err_str);
762     }
763 
764     return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_NETWORK_BIND, "Failed to create libwebsockets context (last OpenSSL error: %s)", ssl_err_str);
765   }
766 
767   // Create thread pool for handling client connections
768   // Use 4 worker threads to handle concurrent client handlers without blocking LWS event loop
769   server->handler_pool = thread_pool_create_with_workers("websocket_handlers", 4);
770   if (!server->handler_pool) {
771     lws_context_destroy(server->context);
772     server->context = NULL;
773     return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_THREAD, "Failed to create WebSocket handler thread pool");
774   }
775 
776   /* Register WebSocket server with named registry */
777   char ws_port_name[32];
778   snprintf(ws_port_name, sizeof(ws_port_name), "ws:%d", server->port);
779   NAMED_REGISTER(server, ws_port_name, "websocket_server", "0x%tx", NULL);
780 
781   /* Register server's sync primitives with hierarchical naming */
782   NAMED_REGISTER_ATOMIC(&server->running, "is_running", (uintptr_t)(const void *)(server));
783 
784   /* Register WebSocket context as child of server */
785   NAMED_REGISTER_WEBSOCKET(server->context, "context", (uintptr_t)(const void *)(server));
786 
787   log_info("WebSocket server initialized on port %d with static file serving", config->port);
788   return ASCIICHAT_OK;
789 }






References atomic_store_bool(),
    lws_log_init_server(), SET_ERRNO, and
    thread_pool_create_with_workers().


Referenced by acds_main(), and server_main().






asciichat_error_t
  websocket_server_run (websocket_server_t * server)


Definition at line 791 of file
    lib/network/websocket/server.c.


791                                                                    {
792   if (!server || !server->context) {
793     return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, "Invalid server");
794   }
795 
796   log_info("WebSocket server starting event loop on port %d", server->port);
797 
798   // Run libwebsockets event loop
799   uint64_t last_service_ns = 0;
800   int service_call_count = 0;
801   while (atomic_load_bool(&server->running)) {
802     // Service libwebsockets with 16ms timeout to match 60 FPS frame rate.
803     // This provides frequent event processing (~60 callback invocations per second) so that
804     // WebSocket frames queued by the render loop are sent promptly, matching TCP performance.
805     // All client connections share this single server context, so a single lws_service() call
806     // services fragments and events for all clients simultaneously.
807     uint64_t service_start_ns = time_get_ns();
808     if (last_service_ns && service_start_ns - last_service_ns > 30 * US_PER_MS_INT) {
809       // > 30ms gap between service calls
810       double gap_ms = (double)(service_start_ns - last_service_ns) / 1e6;
811       log_info_every(1 * US_PER_MS_INT, "[LWS_SERVICE_GAP] %.1fms gap between lws_service calls", gap_ms);
812     }
813     service_call_count++;
814     log_debug_every(500 * US_PER_MS_INT, "[LWS_SERVICE] Call #%d, context=%s", service_call_count,
815                     NAMED_DESCRIBE(server->context, "websocket_server"));
816     int result = lws_service(server->context, 1);
817     if (result < 0) {
818       log_error("libwebsockets service error: %d", result);
819       break;
820     }
821   }
822 
823   log_info(
824       "WebSocket server event loop exited (context will be destroyed by main thread after handler threads complete)");
825   return ASCIICHAT_OK;
826 }






References atomic_load_bool(), SET_ERRNO, and
    time_get_ns().
